settings.title: ACTIVE DIABETIC SOCK COLLECTION
settings.text:
Circufiber Active Diabetic socks are designed for people in pursuit of a healthier, more active lifestyle. Featuring moderate compression in the arch and tube, the Active Collection offers breathable easy on, easy off construction with support where and when you need it.

All Circufiber socks feature our patent pending infrared technology that is clinically proven to improve circulation in the feet and lower legs.

settings.promo_text: Description of promotion with lots of details.

settings.heading: No Risk
settings.description:
For best results, wear Circufiber Diabetic socks for 6+ hours every day for at least 2 weeks. If for any reason you are not 100% satisfied with the results, we will provide you with a free pre-paid return label and your money back.

Read our Happy Feet Guarantee

settings.title: Are your socks made in the USA?
settings.answer: Our socks are designed in America and made with nearly 70 years of craft and expertise in North Carolina.

settings.title: What's the height of your socks?
settings.answer: Sock Height measurements are from the top of the heel pad to the top of the cuff. Height manufacturing tolerances of .5” in either direction are to be expected.

settings.title: Does the IR technology wear out?
settings.answer: We embed our proprietary blend of minerals directly into our Circufiber® yarns, making Circulight® IR virtually indestructible. While no socks last forever, Circulight® IR won't wash out or wear out, giving you the same benefits each and every time you wear them.

settings.title: Are your socks compression socks?
settings.answer: While just about all socks have some degree of “compression” (they wouldn’t stay on your leg otherwise), not all socks are defined as “compression socks.” Compression socks are specially designed to apply pressure to your lower legs, helping to maintain blood flow and reduce discomfort and swelling. At present, we do not sell socks that would be considered “compression socks,” though our clinically-proven, proprietary infrared technology addresses similar conditions and provides many of the same benefits. Always consult with your medical professional if you have been advised to wear compression socks.

settings.title: Are your socks non-skid/slip?
settings.answer: Presently, our socks do not have a non-skid/slip bottom. We will be offering non-skid/slip options in the near future. If you sign up on circufiber.com, you’ll be the first to hear about new products, offers and valuable discounts on Circufiber products.

settings.title: Are your socks covered by health insurance/HSA/FSA/prescriptions?
settings.answer: You do not need a prescription to purchase Circufiber socks. At this time, our socks are not yet covered by insurance, Medicare, Medicaid, FSA/HSA.

settings.title: Will these socks help with my Edema/Charcot Foot/Lymphedema?
settings.answer: Our socks have been clinically proven to increase circulation in the feet and lower legs of people living with diabetes. While we have not studied our socks on people with conditions such as Edema, Charcot foot, etc. we have many customer reviews on circufiber.com that mention benefits for other foot conditions. If you have any questions or concerns about using our products to treat any disease or condition, please consult with your medical professional.
